key_insight: This critical review argues that adding solid lubricants to thermal spray coatings can lower friction and wear while preserving the protective benefits of sprayed layers.
engineering_relevance: For tribological and high-temperature components, these coatings offer a practical route to improve surface durability, but performance depends strongly on material pairings, process conditions, and operating environment.
method: Reviews solid-lubricant-doped thermal spray coating systems reported in the literature.
Compares coating chemistries, spray processes, and friction/wear outcomes.
Synthesizes design guidelines and gaps for future coating development.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal review article indexed by OpenAlex rather than a new experimental dataset, so the evidence is synthesized from prior published studies.
watch_item: A key concern is that lubricant addition can trade off hardness, adhesion, and thermal stability, so coating optimization and validation under service conditions are essential.
paper_title: Solid Lubricant-Doped Thermal Spray Coatings: A Critical Review
